blocks:
Ayahs

وَٱلْعَـٰدِيَـٰتِ ضَبْحًا (۱)
By the snorting chargers, (1)
فَٱلْمُورِيَـٰتِ قَدْحًا (۲)
by the strikers of sparks [with their hoofs], (2)
فَٱلْمُغِيرَٰتِ صُبْحًا (۳)
by the raiders at dawn, (3)
فَأَثَرْنَ بِهِۦ نَقْعًا (٤)
raising therein a trail of dust, (4)
فَوَسَطْنَ بِهِۦ جَمْعًا (٥)
and cleaving therein a host! (5)
إِنَّ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ لِرَبِّهِۦ لَكَنُودٌ (٦)
Indeed man is ungrateful to his Lord, (6)
وَإِنَّهُۥ عَلَىٰ ذَٰلِكَ لَشَهِيدٌ (۷)
and indeed he is [himself] witness to that! (7)
وَإِنَّهُۥ لِحُبِّ ٱلْخَيْرِ لَشَدِيدٌ (۸)
And indeed he is an avid lover of wealth. (8)
۞ أَفَلَا يَعْلَمُ إِذَا بُعْثِرَ مَا فِى ٱلْقُبُورِ (۹)
Does he not know, when what is [buried] in the graves is turned over, (9)
وَحُصِّلَ مَا فِى ٱلصُّدُورِ (۱٠)
and what is [concealed] in the breasts is divulged, (10)
إِنَّ رَبَّهُم بِهِمْ يَوْمَئِذٍ لَّخَبِيرٌۢ (۱۱)
on that day their Lord will be well informed about them [and their deeds]? (11)

Key Points

'Adw' means transgression. Since running is a transgression of the normal limit of movement, it is called 'adw. 'Dabh' means the sound of a horse's breath. 'Qadh' means the emission of fire from flint stone. 'Al-Mughīrāt' (the raiders) is from 'ighārah', meaning swift raiding. 'Subhan' is a metaphor for surprising the enemy and concealing the time of attack. 'Atharn' is from 'ithārah', meaning to spread or scatter. 'Naq' means dust, and 'kanūd' means ungrateful (kafūr).

The word 'bu'thira' contains the meaning of resurrection (ba'th) and scattering (ithārah), meaning to pull out and turn upside down. 'Hussila' means extracting the kernel from the husk, and the distinguishing of good and bad characteristics from one another on that Day.

In some verses, the Quran censures man for certain traits, calling him "ظلوماً جهولاً" (114) (tyrannical and ignorant), "هلوعاً" (115) (impatient/greedy), "یؤوساً" (116) (despairing), "كفوراً" (117) (ungrateful), "جزوعاً" (118) (impatient), and "منوعاً" (119) (stingy). But on the other hand, concerning man, He says: "كرّمنا" (120) (We honored him), "فضّلنا" (121) (We preferred him), "احسن تقویم" (122) (We created him in the best stature), "نفخت فیه من روحى" (123) (We breathed into him of My Spirit). This duality is because man possesses two types of driving forces: one is intellect ('aql) and the other is instinct (gharīzah). If he follows the path of servitude to Allah and the training of Allah's saints, he is one way; and if he follows the path of desires, tyrants, and temptations, he is another way.

The Quran criticizes the intense and greedy attachment to worldly wealth that causes the forgetting of the Hereafter and the deprived. "انّه لحبّ الخیر لشدید" The Quran refers to worldly wealth as 'khayr' (good) in the phrase: "لحبّ الخیر" to make it understood that wealth must be acquired through good means, and spent in good ways, with good intention, and by good methods.

114) Ahzab, 72. 115) Ma'arij, 19. 116) Isra, 83. 117) Isra, 67. 118) Ma'arij, 20. 119) Ma'arij, 21. 120) Isra, 70. 121) Isra, 70. 122) Tin, 4. 123) Hijr, 29.

Messages

1- Jihad and defense are so valuable that Allah swears by the breath of the horses ridden by the fighters. "و العادیات ضَبحا"

2- Muslims must be skilled riders and agile. (The oath by the horse's breath is a form of encouragement for purposeful horsemanship.) "و العادیات ضَبحا"

3- Horses run in the way of Allah, but man shows obstinacy against Allah. "والعادیات... انّ الانسان لربّه لكنود"

4- In Jihad against the enemy, speed of action is a value. "و العادیات ضَبحا"

5- Use the enemy's moment of negligence for attack. "فالمغیرات صُبحا"

6- Surprise the enemy so completely that you are suddenly in their midst, giving them no opportunity to counter you. "فوسطن به جمعًا"

7- Ingratitude and love of wealth place man in opposition to the front of truth. "و العادیات... انّ الانسان لربّه لكنود... لحبّ الخیر لشدید"

8- Do not forget the martyrs of the fronts and the divine aids. "انّ الانسان لربّه لكنود"

9- Victory and security make man arrogant; a warning is necessary. "لربّه لكنود"

10- Man's conscience is aware; even when he offers excuses, he knows what he is doing. "و انّه على ذلك لشهید"

11- In Islamic culture, wealth and property are 'khayr' (good). "انّه لحبّ الخیر لشدید"

12- The attachment to wealth is an innate matter; what is blameworthy is the excessive attachment, which leads man, on the one hand, to seek every kind of income, and on the other hand, to fail to pay the obligatory divine rights. "لشدید"

13- The Resurrection is physical because it speaks of the grave. "بعثر ما فى القبور" (In another verse, He says: "یبعث من فى القبور" (124))

14- The remembrance of the Day of Judgment is a warning factor for the ungrateful and the wealth-worshippers. "أفلا یعلم اذا بعثر ما فى القبور"

15- On the Day of Judgment, inner secrets will be revealed and accounted for. "حصّل ما فى الصّدور"

16- Allah has complete awareness of our thoughts and deeds. ('Khabīr' means awareness of the apparent and the hidden) "انّ ربّهم بهم... لخبیر"

17- Allah is aware of people's affairs even in this world, but on the Day of Judgment, this quality of being All-Aware (Khabīr) becomes manifest to everyone. "یومئذ"

18- Since Allah is All-Aware (Khabīr), His accounting is also precise. "یومئذ لخبیر"

19- Man does not show this much rebellion against others; man's intense disbelief and ingratitude are only against his Lord. "لربّه لكنود" 124) Hajj, 7.
